item.title: Weeknight pasta
item.description: Pasta dishes that hit the table in under 30 minutes without sacrificing technique.

item.title: Black peppercorns
item.description: Dried unripe fruit of Piper nigrum. Heat from piperine; aroma from terpenes that vanish on grinding.

item.title: Butter (unsalted)
item.description: Cow's-milk fat with ~16% water and ~2% milk solids. The solids are the flavor lever in cooking.

item.title: Pecorino romano
item.description: Hard sheep's-milk cheese from Lazio. Salty, sharp, lactic — the canonical cheese for Roman pasta.

item.title: Brown butter cacio e pepe
item.description: Roman three-ingredient pasta, weaponized: brown butter replaces olive oil to layer Maillard nuttiness over the pecorino lactic-tang and bloomed peppercorn piperine.

item.title: Brown butter (beurre noisette)
item.description: Cook butter past the foaming stage until milk solids turn amber — yields hazelnut aroma and Maillard depth from the casein toasting.

item.title: Pan emulsion (mantecare)
item.description: Bind starchy pasta water and fat into a glossy sauce by tossing pasta with fat over low heat, finishing off-heat with cheese.
